response: Let's solve this problem step by step. Without loss of generality, let's assume that \((p+qi)(r+si) = 13+i\) and \((p-qi) + (r-si) = 3+4i\). From the sum of the roots taken two at a time, we know that \(b = (p+qi)(r+si) + (p+qi)(r-si) + (p+qi)(p-qi) + (r+si)(r-si) + (p-qi)(r-si) + (p-qi)(r+si)\). Simplifying, we get \(b = (13+i) + (p+qi)(r-si) + (p-qi)(r+si) + (r+si)(r-si) + (p-qi)(r-si) + (p-qi)(r+si)\). Notice that \((p+qi)(r-si) + (p-qi)(r+si) = 2pr + 2qs\) and \((p-qi)(r-si) + (p-qi)(r+si) = 2pr - 2qs\). Also, \((r+si)(r-si) = r^2 + s^2\). So, \(b = 13 + i + 2pr + 2qs + r^2 + s^2 + 2pr - 2qs + r^2 + s^2 = 13 + i + 4pr + 2r^2 + 2s^2\). Given that \((p-qi) + (r-si) = 3+4i\), we can see that \(p + r = 3\) and \(-q - s = 4\). From \((p+qi)(r+si) = 13+i\), we get \(pr - qs + (ps + qr)i = 13 + i\), so \(pr - qs = 13\) and \(ps + qr = 1\). From \((p-qi) + (r-si) = 3+4i\), we get \(p + r = 3\) and \(-q - s = 4\). So, \(b = 13 + 4pr + 2r^2 + 2s^2\). Since \(p + r = 3\), we can let \(p = 3 - r\). Also, since \(-q - s = 4\), we can let \(q = -4 - s\). Substituting these into \(pr - qs = 13\) and \(ps + qr = 1\), we get \((3-r)r - (-4-s)s = 13\) and \((3-r)s + r(-4-s) = 1\). Solving these equations, we get \(3r - r^2 + 4s + s^2 = 13\) and \(3s - rs - 4r - rs = 1\). Solving these, we get \(r^2 - 3r - s^2 - 4s + 13 = 0\) and \(2rs + 4r + 3s = 1\). Solving these, we get \(r = 2\) and \(s = -1\). So, \(p = 1\) and \(q = -3\). So, \(b = 13 + 4(2)(1) + 2(2^2) + 2(-1^2) = 13 + 8 + 8 + 2 = 31\). </think> <answer> 31 </answer>
